checks.description: After exactly four consecutive valid inputs, valid_out must assert for one cycle and data_out must equal the concrete sum of the driven inputs (1+2+3+4 = 10).
notes: Uses the concrete stimulus defined in the plan; no exact-cycle latency is assumed.
checks.pass_condition: valid_out is low during the first three valid inputs, goes high for exactly one cycle after the fourth input, and data_out equals 10 during that high cycle.
checks.trigger_condition: When the deterministic stimulus drives the four‑sample valid group defined in case basic_002.

checks.description: After four maximum‑width valid inputs, valid_out must assert for one cycle and data_out must equal the concrete sum (255*4 = 1020).
notes: Boundary values are driven by the plan; timing remains event‑based.
checks.pass_condition: valid_out stays low for the first three inputs, goes high for a single cycle after the fourth input, and data_out equals 1020 during that high cycle.
checks.trigger_condition: When the deterministic stimulus drives the four‑sample maximum‑value group defined in case edge_002.

checks.description: Only cycles with valid_in asserted contribute to the accumulation; after the four accepted samples, valid_out must assert once and data_out must equal the sum of the accepted values (1+2+3+4 = 10).
notes: Gapped cycles are ignored per the plan; no exact latency is required.
checks.pass_condition: valid_out remains low throughout all gap cycles, asserts for a single cycle after the fourth accepted sample, and data_out equals 10 during that assertion.
checks.trigger_condition: When the deterministic stimulus with interleaved valid_low gaps drives the pattern defined in case protocol_001.

checks.description: A reset asserted mid‑group must clear any partial accumulation; after reset release, a full group of four valid inputs must produce a single output event with data_out equal to the sum of the post‑reset inputs (5+6+7+8 = 26).
notes: Partial pre‑reset samples are ignored; timing remains event‑based.
checks.pass_condition: No valid_out assertion occurs before the post‑reset group completes; after the fourth post‑reset input, valid_out asserts for one cycle and data_out equals 26.
checks.trigger_condition: When the stimulus sequence defined in case regression_001 drives a partial group, asserts reset, then drives a full post‑reset group.

checks.description: Two consecutive groups of four valid inputs must each generate a distinct output event; the first event must carry the sum of the first group (1+2+3+4 = 10) and the second event must carry the sum of the second group (10+11+12+13 = 46).
notes: The stream is finite; no exact inter‑group latency is assumed.
checks.pass_condition: valid_out stays low until the fourth input of the first group, asserts for one cycle with data_out = 10, then stays low until the fourth input of the second group, asserts for one cycle with data_out = 46.
checks.trigger_condition: When the deterministic stimulus defined in case back_to_back_002 drives two back‑to‑back valid groups.
